classes.name: qtapputils.widgets.range.DoubleSpinBox

methods.name: qtapputils.widgets.range.DoubleSpinBox.keyReleaseEvent
documentation:
Override qt base method to consume key release events so that they are
not propagated to the parent.

documentation:
Override qt base method to consume key press events so that they are
not propagated to the parent.

methods.name: qtapputils.widgets.range.DoubleSpinBox.textFromValue
documentation:
Override qt base method to work around the bug described at
https://bugreports.qt.io/browse/QTBUG-77939 when a ',' is
used as decimal separator.

A standard qt double spinbox that implements a workaround for
the bug described at https://bugreports.qt.io/browse/QTBUG-77939.

classes.name: qtapputils.widgets.range.RangeSpinBox

methods.name: qtapputils.widgets.range.RangeSpinBox.sizeHint
documentation:
Override Qt method to add a buffer to the hint width of the spinbox,
so that there is enough space to hold the maximum value in the spinbox
when editing.

methods.name: qtapputils.widgets.range.RangeSpinBox.fixup
documentation: Override Qt method.

methods.name: qtapputils.widgets.range.RangeSpinBox.validate
documentation: Override Qt method.

A spinbox that allow to enter values that are lower or higher than the
minimum and maximum value of the spinbox.

When editing is finished, the value is corrected to the maximum if the
value entered was above the maximum and to the minimum if it was below
the minimum.

documentation: Return the start value of the range.

methods.name: qtapputils.widgets.range.RangeWidget.end
documentation: Return the end value of the range.

methods.name: qtapputils.widgets.range.RangeWidget.set_range
documentation: Set the start and end value of the range.

methods.name: qtapputils.widgets.range.RangeWidget.set_minimum
documentation: Set the minimum allowed value of the start of the range.

methods.name: qtapputils.widgets.range.RangeWidget.set_maximum
documentation: Set the maximum allowed value of the end of the range.

methods.name: qtapputils.widgets.range.RangeWidget._block_spinboxes_signals
documentation: Block signals from the spinboxes.
---- Private methods and handlers

methods.name: qtapputils.widgets.range.RangeWidget._update_spinbox_range
documentation:
Make sure lowcut and highcut spinbox ranges are
mutually exclusive

methods.name: qtapputils.widgets.range.RangeWidget._handle_value_changed
documentation: Handle when the value of the lowcut or highcut spinbox changed.

A Qt object that link two double spinboxes that can be used to define
the start and end value of a range.

The RangeWidget does not come with a layout and both the spinbox_start
and spinbox_end must be added to a layout independently.

modules.name: qtapputils.widgets.statusbar
documentation: -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

https://github.com/jnsebgosselin/qtapputils
This file is part of QtAppUtils.
Licensed under the terms of the MIT License.
---- Third party imports

classes.name: qtapputils.widgets.statusbar.ProcessStatusBar

methods.name: qtapputils.widgets.statusbar.ProcessStatusBar.__init__
documentation:
A process status bar including an icon and a label.

Parameters
----------
parent : QWidget, optional
    The parent of the progress status bar. The default is None.
iconsize : int, optional
    The size of the icon. The default is 24.
ndots : int, optional
    Number of dots to use for the spinner icon . The default is 11.
orientation : int, optional
    Orientation of the progress status bar. The default is
    Qt.Horizontal.
spacing : in, optional
    Spacing between the icon and the label. The default is 5.
contents_margin : list[int], optional
    A list of four integers corresponding to the left, top, right, and
    bottom contents margin. The default is 0 on all sides.
hsize_policy : str, optional
    An attribute describing horizontal resizing policy. Valid
    values are 'minimum' or 'expanding'. This option is ignored
    when the orientation of the progress bar is horizontal.
vsize_policy : str, optional
    An attribute describing vertical resizing policy. Valid
    values are 'minimum', 'expanding', or 'minimum_expanding'.
text_valign : str, optional
    The vertical alignment of the text. De default is 'center'.
    Valid values are 'top', 'bottom', or 'center'.
icon_valign : str, optional
    The vertical alignment of the icon. De default is 'center'.
    Valid values are 'top', 'bottom', or 'center'.

methods.name: qtapputils.widgets.statusbar.ProcessStatusBar.show_icon
documentation: Show icon named 'icon_name' and hide all other icons.

methods.name: qtapputils.widgets.statusbar.ProcessStatusBar.hide_icons
documentation: Hide all icons.

methods.name: qtapputils.widgets.statusbar.ProcessStatusBar.set_icon
documentation: Set the icon named 'name'.

methods.name: qtapputils.widgets.statusbar.ProcessStatusBar.set_label
documentation: Set the text that is displayed next to the spinner.

methods.name: qtapputils.widgets.statusbar.ProcessStatusBar.show_update_icon
documentation: Stop and hide the spinner and show an update icon instead.

methods.name: qtapputils.widgets.statusbar.ProcessStatusBar.show_fail_icon
documentation: Stop and hide the spinner and show a failed icon instead.

methods.name: qtapputils.widgets.statusbar.ProcessStatusBar.show_sucess_icon
documentation: Stop and hide the spinner and show a success icon instead.

methods.name: qtapputils.widgets.statusbar.ProcessStatusBar.show
documentation: Extend Qt method to start the waiting spinner.

methods.name: qtapputils.widgets.statusbar.ProcessStatusBar.hide
documentation: Extend Qt hide to stop waiting spinner.

A status bar that shows the progression status and results of a process.

This class was taken from the Sardes project.
See sardes/widgets/statusbar.py at https://github.com/geo-stack/sardes

Add a task to the stack that will be executed when the thread of

documentation: Execute the tasks that were added to the stack.
A worker to execute tasks without blocking the GUI.

classes.name: qtapputils.managers.taskmanagers.TaskManagerBase

methods.name: qtapputils.managers.taskmanagers.TaskManagerBase.run_tasks
documentation:
Execute all the tasks that were added to the stack.

Parameters
----------
callback : Callable, optional
    A callback that will be called with the provided returned_values
    after the current queued tasks have been all executed.
returned_values : tuple, optional
    A list of values that will be passed to the callback function when
    it is called.

methods.name: qtapputils.managers.taskmanagers.TaskManagerBase.add_task
documentation: Add a new task at the end of the queued tasks stack.

methods.name: qtapputils.managers.taskmanagers.TaskManagerBase.worker
documentation: Return the worker that is installed on this manager.

methods.name: qtapputils.managers.taskmanagers.TaskManagerBase.set_worker
documentation: "Install the provided worker on this manager

methods.name: qtapputils.managers.taskmanagers.TaskManagerBase._handle_task_completed
documentation:
Handle when a task has been completed by the worker.

This is the ONLY slot that should be called after a task is
completed by the worker.
---- Private API

methods.name: qtapputils.managers.taskmanagers.TaskManagerBase._cleanup_task
documentation: Cleanup task associated with the specified UUID.

methods.name: qtapputils.managers.taskmanagers.TaskManagerBase._add_task
documentation: Add a new task at the end of the stack of queued tasks.

methods.name: qtapputils.managers.taskmanagers.TaskManagerBase._run_tasks
documentation: Execute all the tasks that were added to the stack of queued tasks.

methods.name: qtapputils.managers.taskmanagers.TaskManagerBase._run_pending_tasks
documentation: Execute all pending tasks.
A basic FIFO (First-In, First-Out) task manager.

classes.name: qtapputils.managers.taskmanagers.LIFOTaskManager

methods.name: qtapputils.managers.taskmanagers.LIFOTaskManager._add_task
documentation:
Override method so that the tasks are managed as a LIFO
stack (Last-in, First out) instead of FIFO (First-In, First-Out).

A last-in, first out (LIFO) task manager manager, where there's always
at most one task in the queue, and if a new task is added, it overrides
or replaces the existing task.

modules.name: qtapputils.managers.fileio
documentation: -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
Copyright © QtAppUtils Project Contributors
https://github.com/jnsebgosselin/apputils
This file is part of QtAppUtils.
Licensed under the terms of the MIT License.

methods.name: qtapputils.managers.fileio.SaveFileManager.__init__
documentation:
A manager to save files.

Parameters
----------
namefilters : dict
    A dictionary containing the file filters to use in the
    'Save As' file dialog. Here is an example of a correctly
    formated namefilters dictionary:

        namefilters = {
            '.pdf': 'Portable Document Format (*.pdf)',
            '.svg': 'Scalable Vector Graphics (*.svg)',
            '.png': 'Portable Network Graphics (*.png)',
            '.jpg': 'Joint Photographic Expert Group (*.jpg)'
            }

    Note that the first entry in the dictionary will be used as the
    default name filter to use in the 'Save As' dialog.
onsave : Callable
    The callable that is used to save the file.
parent: QWidget, optional
    The parent widget to use for the 'Save As' file dialog.

methods.name: qtapputils.managers.fileio.SaveFileManager.save_file
documentation:
Save in provided filename.

Parameters
----------
filename : str
    The abosulte path where to save the file.

Returns
-------
filename : str
    The absolute path where the file was successfully saved. Returns
    'None' if the saving operation was cancelled or was unsuccessfull.

methods.name: qtapputils.managers.fileio.SaveFileManager.save_file_as
documentation:
Save in a new file.

Parameters
----------
filename : dict
    The default or suggested absolute path where to save the file.

Returns
-------
filename : str
    The absolute path where the file was successfully saved. Returns
    'None' if the saving operation was cancelled or was unsuccessfull.

A Qt object to catch exceptions and emit a formatted string of the error.

classes.name: qtapputils.managers.capture.StandardStreamEmitter
documentation:
A Qt object to intercept and emit the input and output of the
Python interpreter.

https://docs.python.org/3/library/sys.html#sys.stdout
https://docs.python.org/3/library/sys.html#sys.stderr

classes.name: qtapputils.managers.capture.SysCaptureManager

methods.name: qtapputils.managers.capture.SysCaptureManager.start_capture
documentation:
Start capturing Python interpreter standard messages and unhandled
raised exceptions.

Important Note:
    the system capture manager should NOT be started when testing
    under pytest because this will cause problems with the way
    pytest is already capturing standard system messages and
    raised exceptions.

    See pytest/src/_pytest/capture.py

methods.name: qtapputils.managers.capture.SysCaptureManager.stop_capture
documentation:
Stop capturing Python interpreter standard messages and unhandled
raised exceptions.

methods.name: qtapputils.managers.capture.SysCaptureManager.register_stdstream_console
documentation: Register the specified console to this system capture manager.

methods.name: qtapputils.managers.capture.SysCaptureManager.register_except_dialog
documentation: Register the system except dialog.

methods.name: qtapputils.managers.capture.SysCaptureManager.handle_stderr
documentation: Handle Python interpreter standard errors.

methods.name: qtapputils.managers.capture.SysCaptureManager.__handle_stdout
documentation: Handle Python interpreter standard output.

methods.name: qtapputils.managers.capture.SysCaptureManager._handle_except
documentation:
Handle raised exceptions that have not been handled properly
internally and need to be reported for bug fixing.

A manager to capture and manage Python's standard input and output
streams, logging and internal errors reporting.

Important Note:
    the system capture manager should NOT be started when testing
    under pytest because this will cause problems with the way
    pytest is already capturing standard system messages and
    raised exceptions.

    See pytest/src/_pytest/capture.py

functions.name: qtapputils.qthelpers.create_waitspinner
documentation:
Create a wait spinner.

Parameters
----------
size : int, optional
    The size of the spinner in integer point precision (one point is equal
    to 1/72 of an inch). The default is 32.
n : int, optional
    The number of dots used to draw the spinner. The default is 11.
parent : QWidget, optional
    The parent widget of the spinner. The default is None.

Returns
-------
spinner : WaitingSpinner
    The waitspinner created with the specified parameters

functions.name: qtapputils.qthelpers.format_statustip
documentation:
Format text and shortcut into a single str to be set
as an action status tip. The status tip is displayed on all status
bars provided by the action's top-level parent widget.

functions.name: qtapputils.qthelpers.format_tooltip
documentation:
Format text, tip and shortcut into a single str to be set
as a widget's tooltip.
